document_info.prepared_by: David Reinhardt, Vice President, Transportation Insurance Specialists

business_description.nature_of_business: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors

coverages_discussed.notes: Workers' Compensation coverage is provided through Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation per state statute.
Occurrence form, including standard premises/operations and products/completed operations.
Business Auto covering all owned, hired, and non-owned vehicles.
Coverage for all scheduled locations on a special form with replacement cost valuation.
Following form over primary GL, Auto, and Employer's Liability.
Motor Truck Cargo coverage as per expiring program.

positives: Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually
Dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits
Employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative in place for over five years
Contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements
Third-party risk control survey completed within the past 12 months with favorable results
Formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%
All locations equipped with security camera systems and 24/7 monitoring services
485 employees with structured HR program, low turnover and consistent safety training
Fleet of 37 vehicles maintained under a documented preventive maintenance schedule
concerns: Loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period
Annotation indicates 'Need docs' for supporting documentation
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over the review period, 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 paid and $1,510,580.11 incurred; 13 claims open, 35 closed. Annualized loss ratio 12.9% (avg annual incurred $302,116.02 vs annual premium $2,333,947.33 over 5 loss years). Breakdown: Workers' Compensation 24 claims ($826,622.56 paid / $896,295.98 incurred); General Liability 5 claims ($54,126.51 paid / $54,126.51 incurred); Commercial Auto 16 claims ($387,376.46 paid / $449,550.02 incurred); Commercial Property 2 claims ($106,691.36 paid / $106,691.36 incurred); Umbrella/Excess 0 claims; Motor Truck Cargo 1 claim ($3,916.24 paid / $3,916.24 incurred).

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Great Lakes Freight Systems, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus, with approximately 38 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property, Umbrella / Excess, Motor Truck Cargo.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: Regional trucking and freight logistics — LTL and FTL general freight, warehousing and distribution services across the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic corridors, headquartered in Columbus.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually; dedicated safety committee meeting monthly with quarterly site audits; employee assistance program and workplace wellness initiative in place for over five years; contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor/vendor agreements; third-party risk control survey completed in past 12 months with favorable results; formal return-to-work program reducing lost-time claims by over 30%; security camera systems and 24/7 monitoring at all locations; structured HR program with consistent safety training; documented preventive maintenance schedule for fleet of 37 vehicles.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The loss experience reflects a mixed trend with some large individual claims in the review period. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 48 total claims with $1,378,733.13 in total paid losses and $1,510,580.11 total incurred. Currently, 13 claims remain open and 35 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 12.9% (avg annual incurred $302,116.02 against current annual premium $2,333,947.33 over 5 loss years).
